markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Cape Town be 16°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Cape Town International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Cape Town International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/za/matroosfontein/FACT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies an 18°C high for Cape Town on May 11 at 45% probability, driven by the latest South African Weather Service (SAWS) forecasts and global model ensembles like ECMWF and GFS, which project limited daytime heating under persistent cloud cover and northwest winds gusting over 50 km/h from a recent cold front. These conditions suppress maximum temperatures below the early-May climatological average of 19–20°C, as seen in historical data where similar autumnal systems reduce solar insolation by 30–50%. Probabilities taper to 19°C (28%) and 17°C (26%), capturing ±1°C uncertainty in model runs and potential afternoon clearing. SAWS updates and real-time airport observations at Cape Town International will refine outcomes as the day progresses.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no announcement by June 30 at 50.1% following President Trump's April 2 firing of Attorney General Pam Bondi and appointment of loyal defense attorney Todd Blanche as acting AG, with reports indicating Trump satisfaction with his performance and legal flexibility to maintain an acting leader for months without Senate confirmation. EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin leads nominees at 13% amid pre-firing discussions of his elevation, bolstered by his Trump allegiance and prior cabinet consideration. Blanche trails at 8% on his interim role and personal representation of Trump in trials; Texas AG Ken Paxton at 6.3% reflects his aggressive stances against federal overreach; Harmeet Dhillon at 5.5% draws from her election law expertise. Senate dynamics and nomination timeline loom as pivotal factors.

markets.question: Will Mahmoud Ahmadinejad be head of state in Iran end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Iran on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Iran’s territory at the specified time.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will François Hollande win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

LOOK MUM NO COMPUTER's quirky synth-heavy "Eins, Zwei, Drei"—a March national selection pick blending homemade electronics and German countdowns—has drawn mixed rehearsal reactions for its niche appeal, echoing the UK's historical televote struggles despite Big Five auto-qualification. Haaland's clinical finishing and Manchester City's title-contending form, including recent wins bolstering his goal output, have solidified trader consensus, as evidenced by his pull-ahead in the past week amid Thiago's slight dip. Favorable home fixtures against lower-table sides further entrench his position. Realistic challenges include a Haaland injury sidelining him, or Thiago netting hat-tricks in Brentford's finale while Haaland blanks, though such upsets remain improbable given historical end-of-season trends.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no US acquisition of any part of Greenland in 2026 at 86.5%, reflecting firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections of sovereignty transfer amid legal and diplomatic barriers. President Trump's early-year revival of the idea—echoing his 2019 proposal—sparked brief talks on increased US military presence and tariff threats, but Denmark's foreign minister deemed seizure "absolutely not necessary," while Greenland leaders affirmed self-determination under Danish oversight. A reported US backdown in March, influenced by NATO chief Mark Rutte, halted momentum, with no verifiable progress in the past 30 days despite unconfirmed rumors of renewed discussions. Arctic strategic interests persist, but international norms and lack of willing sellers keep probabilities low.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70%-plus victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention in late April secured the party's official endorsement and propelled his trader consensus to an 81% implied probability for the September 1 Republican gubernatorial primary, reflecting delegate preference for the former Abiomed CEO and major GOP donor amid a narrowed field. Former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy fell short of the 15% delegate threshold to qualify automatically and suspended his campaign days later, dropping his odds to 0.7% as primary voters now focus on Minogue versus Brian Shortsleeve, who cleared the ballot threshold but faces mounting pressure from state GOP chairs urging him to exit for party unity against Democratic incumbent Maura Healey. Shortsleeve's 13.3% standing persists amid his calls for debates, though no recent primary polls have emerged to challenge Minogue's momentum ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slovenia's National Assembly remains without a government following the closely contested March 22 parliamentary election, which produced a hung parliament with no bloc securing the 46 seats needed for a majority. Incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement (GS) won 29 seats but failed coalition talks by April 20 and shifted to opposition, while runner-up Janez Janša's right-wing SDS (28 seats) eyes a centre-right alliance with NSi-SLS-Fokus (9 seats) and Democrats (6 seats) for 43 seats, potentially bolstered by external support from Resni.ca (5 seats). President Nataša Pirc Musar skipped an initial prime minister nomination on April 25 amid stalled negotiations; the second consultation round began May 6, with a prospective confidence vote looming. Failure to form a coalition could trigger snap elections within months.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Spider-Man: Brand New Day as the 2026 highest-grossing film at 65.5% implied probability, propelled by its record-shattering trailer debut—718.6 million views in 24 hours last March, the biggest ever—and surging presales tracking for a July 31 release that evokes No Way Home's $1.9 billion benchmark. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie leads year-to-date at around $940 million worldwide after a strong April launch, but weakening legs amid competition from holdovers like Michael and newcomers like The Devil Wears Prada 2 signal a projected finish below $1.2 billion. Avengers: Doomsday trails at 18.5% with massive Phase Six hype, though its December 18 slot clashes with Dune: Messiah, capping its runway; watch summer tracking updates and Toy Story 5's June rollout for momentum shifts.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Turkey" or "Turkiye" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's May 2 speech at the Forum Club of the Palm Beaches triggered a surge in trader consensus for "Dog," as he repeatedly referenced rejecting a White House pet tradition as "fake," criticized Biden's dog Commander for 28 Secret Service bites, and mocked the optics of presidents adopting dogs for votes. This follows a May 1 "Shark" mention—tied to his staple electric boat-electrocution anecdote—that locked in that outcome's certainty. Markets price his rhetorical habits from rallies, interviews, and Truth Social posts, with "Cat" lingering from 2024 immigration claims but unmentioned lately. Upcoming public appearances through May 31 could activate other contracts like "Bear" or "Lion" from cognitive test stories.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 73.5% for a U.S. AI safety bill—requiring risk assessments, safety testing, or transparency mandates for high-risk systems—being signed into law before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress amid partisan divides and the Trump administration's deregulatory stance. Recent reintroductions like the bipartisan Cantwell-Young-Hickenlooper-Blackburn bill to codify the NIST AI Safety Institute and April 2026 proposals tying AI to child safety have advanced little beyond committees, following two failed attempts to preempt state regulations such as California's AI safety law. With the 119th Congress facing 2026 midterms and a White House framework urging minimal burdens on innovation, enactment faces significant procedural hurdles including floor votes and reconciliation challenges before year-end.

markets.question: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Millwall FC and Hull City AFC, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Millwall FC and Hull City AFC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the EFL Championship play-off semi-final second leg on May 11 at The Den, 3rd-placed Millwall host 6th-placed Hull City with promotion to the Premier League at stake, following the first leg at the MKM Stadium on May 8. Millwall's 3-1 away victory over Hull on March 7—thanks to goals from Jake Cooper, Macaulay Ivanović, and Josh Coburn—highlights their recent head-to-head edge and strong away form late in the season. Injury concerns persist for both: Hull sidelined by Eliot Matazo's ACL tear and Cody Drameh's absence, while Millwall monitors Josh Coburn's fitness and Caleb Taylor's foot issue. Millwall's higher finish grants home advantage in this decisive leg, amid high-stakes tension and packed schedules.

events.title: Gaziantep FK vs. Rams Başakşehir FK
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between Gaziantep FK and Rams Başakşehir FK.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin Süper Lig contest at Gaziantep Stadium, with draw priced at 51.5% amid Gaziantep FK's home edge offsetting İstanbul Başakşehir FK's stronger table position (6th vs. 11th) and superior recent form. Gaziantep slumped to a third straight defeat, 2-1 at Göztepe last weekend, scoring sparingly while grappling with injuries to forward Yusuf Kabadayi and defender Salem M'Bakata (cruciate ligament tear), alongside earlier absences like Mustafa Burak Bozan. Başakşehir arrives buoyant from a 3-0 home clean sheet over Samsunspor and 12 goals across their last five outings (three wins, one draw), but their away record tempers favoritism. Head-to-head history favors the visitors (10 wins to four), yet these mid-table clashes frequently stalemate, bunching probabilities tightly around 50% apiece.

events.title: CF Pachuca vs. Deportivo Toluca FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Liga MX game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between CF Pachuca and Deportivo Toluca FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pachuca's dominant 98.8% implied probability stems from their 1-0 first-leg quarterfinal victory at Toluca in the Liga MX Clausura 2026 Liguilla, bolstered by home advantage at Estadio Hidalgo and early second-leg dominance with Enner Valencia's 10th-minute penalty and Robert Kenedy's 48th-minute strike for a 2-0 halftime lead (3-0 aggregate). Toluca, the bicampeones, face key absences like Paulinho, Federico Pereira, Vega, and Gallardo, hampering their attack despite hitting the posts, while Pachuca's defensive resilience reflects superior recent form. A Toluca comeback requires two late unanswered goals for a draw or win, but trader consensus deems this improbable barring red cards, injuries, or extraordinary rally.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Israel decrease the Bank of Israel Interest Rate after the May decision?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the Bank of Israel Interest Rate resulting from the Bank of Israel’s May monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Israel after its May 25, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Bank of Israel interest rate decision schedule: https://www.boi.org.il/en/economic-roles/monetary-policy/interest-rate-announcement-dates-2025-2026/#

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Israel's announcement of their May 25, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the Bank of Israel Interest Rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Israel Decision in May?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the Bank of Israel Interest Rate resulting from the Bank of Israel’s May monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Israel after its May 25, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Bank of Israel interest rate decision schedule: https://www.boi.org.il/en/economic-roles/monetary-policy/interest-rate-announcement-dates-2025-2026/#

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Israel's announcement of their May 25, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the Bank of Israel Interest Rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 74.5% implied probability to a Bank of Israel benchmark interest rate decrease at the May 25, 2026 Monetary Committee meeting, driven by March CPI inflation easing to 1.9% year-over-year—below the 1-3% target—and a 0.2% contraction in economic activity amid the protracted Iran conflict. The policy rate has remained at 4.00% since January's 25 basis point cut, with the Bank's staff forecast projecting 1.7% inflation, creating wide room for easing despite trimmed 2026 GDP growth estimates of 3.3-3.8%. No change at 25.5% reflects war-related uncertainty, while hikes at 0.4% face negligible support given subdued price pressures and shekel strength; April CPI data due mid-May remains a pivotal pre-meeting catalyst.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Abdul El-Sayed at 51.5% to win Michigan's Democratic Senate primary on August 4, ahead of Mallory McMorrow (32%) and Haley Stevens (21.3%), reflecting his strong grassroots fundraising, endorsements from Bernie Sanders and Ro Khanna, and appeal to younger voters under 40 amid generational divides noted in recent Emerson polling. Late April surveys like Detroit News/Glengariff showed Stevens with a narrow edge over El-Sayed in a statistical toss-up, with McMorrow trailing and high undecideds, yet markets diverge by pricing El-Sayed higher due to progressive momentum in this open-seat race vacated by retiring Sen. Gary Peters. Intensifying attacks highlighted in early May reporting underscore the combative three-way contest, with no major shifts in the past week.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $296 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon's share price hovers near $273, reflecting trader consensus buoyed by the April 29 Q1 2026 earnings beat, with revenue up 17% year-over-year to $181.5 billion, AWS accelerating 28% to $37.6 billion on surging AI infrastructure demand, and operating margins hitting a record 13.1% from cost discipline and high-margin cloud growth. The stock touched a 52-week high of $278.56 on May 5 amid post-earnings optimism, though recent consolidation signals caution amid elevated capex projections nearing $200 billion annually for AI. Analyst average price targets cluster around $313, implying modest upside. Key near-term catalysts include today's Amazon Upfront for advertising insights and the May 20 shareholder meeting, with May-end resolution sensitive to Nasdaq trends and Fed policy signals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kristoffer Reitan captured his first PGA Tour title at the Truist Championship, finishing at 15-under to edge Rickie Fowler and Nicolai Højgaard, who tied for second at 13-under, while Alex Fitzpatrick placed fourth at 12-under and Tommy Fleetwood, Sungjae Im, and J.J. Spaun shared fifth at 11-under. Heavy rain delays early in the week softened Quail Hollow's demanding parkland layout, enabling low rounds like Matt McCarty's opening 63 course record and Fowler's final 65 charge from T9. Pre-tournament withdrawals by Scottie Scheffler and Collin Morikawa due to rest and injury concerns opened the field for mid-tier players, with Rory McIlroy's third-round 75 drop from contention to T19 underscoring the leaderboard volatility that shaped trader consensus on top-5 probabilities.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Minnesota Vikings in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: David Njoku's impending free agency departure from the Cleveland Browns—announced in February after his contract expired—has left trader consensus fragmented, with Arizona Cardinals, Los Angeles Chargers, Baltimore Ravens, Denver Broncos, and Indianapolis Colts clustered tightly at 46-46.5% implied probabilities. Recent developments, including Njoku's May visit to the Chargers and March trip to the Ravens, alongside post-2026 NFL Draft rumors linking him to the Broncos, Colts, Titans, and others seeking pass-catching tight end upgrades, underscore the competitive dynamics. The Browns' signing of a veteran replacement and $24.3M dead cap hit have plummeted their odds to 24.5%, reflecting Njoku's stated intent to test the market amid multiple suitors' tight end needs.

Santos' mixed recent form (two wins, one draw, two losses in last five) faces challenges from injuries to Rony, Mayke, Gabriel Menino, and Vinicius Lira, prompting improvisation at right-back despite Neymar and Gabigol availability in a projected 4-2-3-1 under Cuca. Coritiba, with one win and four draws lately, misses Keno, Pedro Morisco, and Rodrigo Rodrigues, relying on Pedro Rocha up top amid solid away resilience. Traders eye key matchups, potential clean sheets, and aggregate implications in this knockout tie.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election held April 16, official results confirm Democrat Analilia Mejia's landslide victory over Republican Joe Hathaway by a 20-25% margin, with Mejia at 60% (81,825 votes) to Hathaway's 39% (53,520 votes) amid 136,000 ballots cast—record turnout for a Thursday contest. This aligns with trader consensus, driven by the district's Democratic lean, Mejia's two-to-one fundraising advantage, progressive endorsements, and strong suburban performance exceeding prior benchmarks like Gov. Mikie Sherrill's margins. No disputes or recounts emerged post-election; final canvass on May 4 solidified tallies. Barring improbable provisional ballot surges or certification anomalies, higher uncertainty reflects the 21-point lead's stability.

markets.question: Will a dozen eggs cost between $2.75–3.00 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the bracket within which the price for "Eggs, Grade A, Large (Cost per Dozen) in U.S. City Average" lies when the April data point is published by the St. Louis Fed (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/APU0000708111).

The St. Louis Fed bases its numbers for egg prices on the BLS's CPI release. The April release is presently scheduled for May 12, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon the update of the St. Louis Fed's chart. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The resolution source for this market measures prices to the third decimal place. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 66% implied probability for the April 2026 U.S. average retail price of a dozen Grade A large eggs falling in the $2.25–$2.50 range, anchored by the Bureau of Labor Statistics' March 2026 reading of $2.348—down from February's $2.50 amid easing highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) pressures and flock rebuilding. USDA Egg Markets Overview data as of May 1 shows steady national supplies and ad prices around $2.58, supporting modest further softening into April, while wholesale benchmarks have plunged over 95% year-over-year from 2025 peaks. A 26.5% chance on $2.00–$2.25 reflects expectations of continued supply-driven declines, with official BLS April data release imminent as the key resolution catalyst.

markets.question: Will Olympiacos win the 2025-26 Euroleague?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club wins the 2025-2026 Euroleague season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to win the 2025-2026 Euroleague per the rules of the Euroleague (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Euroleague (https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Fenerbahce Beko (25.5%) recently wrapped up their best-of-five series over Zalgiris Kaunas, positioning them for a high-stakes Final Four semifinal clash with Olympiacos. Panathinaikos (25.0%), the seventh seed, maintains a 3-1 series lead over No. 2 Valencia Basket (8.0%) despite dropping Game 4 two days ago, with decisive Game 5 looming May 13; their playoff momentum bolsters odds amid Greek rivalry intensity. Real Madrid (23.0%) advances as consistent contenders, awaiting the Pana-Valencia winner.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 71.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, reflecting UEFA's 16 qualifiers packed with elite contenders like Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, Netherlands, and Croatia—bolstered by a flawless qualification path including early April playoffs that confirmed Sweden, Turkey, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Czechia. South America's 20.5% share stems from defending champions Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay, Colombia, and Ecuador topping CONMEBOL's grueling round-robin, maintaining their knockout pedigree despite fewer slots. Africa's 3.5% (Morocco, Senegal, Egypt, Algeria) and Asia's 3.4% (Japan, South Korea, Iran) highlight emerging threats amid expanded fields, while North America's hosts (USA, Canada, Mexico) and Oceania's New Zealand lag due to historical gaps, with the group draw two weeks ago introducing balanced matchups but no seismic shifts.

markets.question: Iran leadership change by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei, ceases to be the de facto leader of Iran at any point between market creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Mojtaba Khamenei will be considered to no longer be the de facto leader of Iran if he is removed from power, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from acting as the de facto leader of Iran within this market's timeframe.

An official announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei’s resignation or removal will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of when the announced departure goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mojtaba Khamenei, appointed Supreme Leader after his father Ali's death in early 2026 US-Israeli airstrikes amid the ongoing Iran war, faces persistent health rumors from severe facial burns and leg injuries sustained in the same attacks, prompting speculation despite official denials of incapacity. President Masoud Pezeshkian confirmed meeting him on May 7 in stable condition, while recent statements affirm his remote involvement in governance and review of US peace proposals. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps maintains strong influence over stability, with no verified challenges to his de facto leadership. Traders price low near-term change probabilities, reflecting regime cohesion during ceasefire negotiations and absent public appearances since April. Upcoming US-Iran talks and potential military developments could sway dynamics.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Peng" during events with Xi Jinping?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping from May 14 to May 15, 2026 (https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/04/china/china-us-talks-iran-intl-hnk). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump is set to hold bilateral meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026, including a state banquet and working lunch, marking their first joint events since October 2025. Trader sentiment hinges on these broadcast appearances amid heightened US-China tensions over Iran sanctions, where recent US Treasury designations on May 8 targeted entities linked to Iranian missiles and prompted China's May 2 anti-sanctions blocking order for refineries buying Iranian oil, alongside Strait of Hormuz disruptions from the ongoing conflict. Pre-summit economic consultations in South Korea on May 12-13 preview discussions on tariffs, rare earth supplies, AI restrictions, chips, and soybeans, with Trump's off-the-cuff style—evident in recent praise of Xi as a "friend"—shaping expectations for key phrase mentions in speeches or Q&A.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman’s strong reelection bid in Wisconsin’s 6th Congressional District, rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report and Safe Republican by Sabato’s Crystal Ball, drives trader consensus heavily toward the GOP at 82.5%. The district’s R+8 Cook Partisan Voter Index and Grothman’s history of double-digit general election margins—61% in 2024—bolster this positioning, amid a fragmented Democratic primary featuring eight candidates, none with significant fundraising to challenge his $700,000 cash on hand as of late March. Recent FEC filings highlight Bradley Smith’s modest grassroots momentum among Democrats, but no polls exist yet in this early cycle ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Independents like firefighter Michael Thurow add minor uncertainty, though structural advantages favor Republicans absent major shifts.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's early April 2026 threats to withdraw the US from NATO—triggered by European allies' refusal to provide bases or ships for US operations in the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions—have fueled market volatility, yet trader consensus assigns low implied probabilities to any exit before year-end. A 2023 law prohibits unilateral presidential withdrawal, mandating two-thirds Senate approval or congressional action, with bipartisan lawmakers from both parties voicing firm opposition to departure. The Pentagon's May 2 announcement of pulling 5,000 troops from Germany over 6-12 months underscores alliance strains but signals repositioning rather than full disengagement. An upcoming White House meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte may address commitments, while no formal denunciation of the North Atlantic Treaty has occurred.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices climbed to $2.67/MMBtu as of May 4, 2026, buoyed by a smaller-than-expected 63 Bcf storage build for the week ending May 1—below the 72 Bcf consensus—amid monthly output declines and near-record LNG exports. Elevated inventories at 2,205 Bcf, well above five-year averages, reflect ample supply entering the injection season, tempering trader optimism despite recent futures rallies to three-week highs around $2.75. EIA forecasts 2% marketed production growth for 2026, supported by Permian gains, while milder shoulder-season weather curbs power sector demand. Traders eye the May 15 EIA storage report and emerging summer cooling forecasts as pivotal for price volatility through month-end.

Incumbent Wesley Hunt vacated for a U.S. Senate bid, but March 3 primary results advanced Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck (47.7%) and Shelly deZevallos (18.6%) to the May 26 GOP runoff, both boasting superior fundraising—Bonck raised $1.47 million and deZevallos $930,000 through March 31, dwarfing Democrat Melissa McDonough's $48,000. Forecasters like Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball rate it Safe or Solid Republican, with Republicans outspending Democrats by millions amid low Democratic primary turnout. The November 3 general election faces no major shifts barring surprises.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 24.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 4:00PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 25 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Cloud9 vs FlyQuest (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 4:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 win the match against FlyQuest.

This market will resolve to "FlyQuest" if FlyQuest win the match against Cloud9.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cloud9's undefeated 5-0 series record and 10-3 games through LCS 2026 Spring Week 5, capped by a hard-fought 2-1 win over Dignitas, positions them as the league's benchmark with elite macro execution from Blaber and Berserker. FlyQuest ranks fourth at 3-2 series (8-5 games), building momentum via 2-1 triumph over Shopify Rebellion and 2-0 rout of Dignitas, though a 1-2 loss to Sentinels exposed vulnerabilities against structured play. Cloud9 holds a commanding 68% historical series win rate in 37 head-to-heads. Full rosters for both in this Week 6 BO3, where a C9 victory locks top seeding contention ahead of playoffs for the top six.

markets.question: Will Jimmy Gomez advance from the CA-34 primary election?
markets.description:
The California primary is currently scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

This market will resolve "Yes" if the listed candidate advances from the primary to contest the seat for California's 34th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominees before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Alavés".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Alavés vs. FC Barcelona - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga standings with a dominant record of 28 wins, 1 draw, and 4 losses after 33 matches, extending their advantage to 11 points over Real Madrid amid a strong run of form. Mid-table Alavés, sitting ninth with 36 points, host the title-chasers at Estadio de Mendizorroza on May 13 in Jornada 36, leveraging home advantage where they've earned solid results. Barcelona's attack remains potent despite season-ending injuries to Lamine Yamal (hamstring) and Andreas Christensen (ACL), with Ronald Araujo nearing return from hamstring discomfort and Marc Bernal recovering from an ankle sprain. Alavés boast no major absences but face stylistic challenges against Barça's possession dominance, as seen in Barcelona's comeback 3-1 win in November; end-of-season rotation and fatigue could sway dynamics.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 0 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from May 4 - 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Geological Survey data confirms zero earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or greater worldwide from May 4-10, 2026, with global seismic networks detecting only smaller events like a M5.2 near Silver Springs, Nevada on May 1, solidifying trader consensus at 95% implied probability for zero outcomes. This aligns with historical seismicity patterns, where USGS records show roughly 50-80 such events annually—about one per week on average—making null weeks feasible amid Poisson-distributed activity along active fault lines. The residual 5% on one or more reflects minor uncertainty from potential post-event magnitude revisions, as preliminary measures can adjust slightly upon review, though no borderline quakes (e.g., M6.4) occurred to prompt this. Final USGS catalog updates expected soon could lock in resolution.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs Anthropic at 96.2% implied probability for the best coding AI model by May's end, driven by its Claude series—particularly Claude Mythos Preview and Opus 4.7—dominating key benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified (93.9%) and Pro (77.8%). Challenges could arise from surprise pre-deadline releases, such as an OpenAI GPT-5.4 update or Google Gemini advancement, though timelines and benchmark gaps make shifts unlikely absent major announcements.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 22°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 21°C high in Milan at 32.5% implied probability for May 12, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts converging on 20-22°C maxima under a building high-pressure ridge following today's overcast conditions and possible thunderstorms around 19°C. Key variables include afternoon cloud cover persistence, which could cap temperatures at 19-20°C, versus full solar insolation and light southerly winds boosting to 22°C via urban heat island effects; northerly gusts or lingering moisture favor lower outcomes like 18°C (10.8%), while rare clear skies enable 23°C+ tails (<5%). Spring model spread reflects inherent short-range uncertainty, with new 12Z runs expected midday May 11 to sharpen guidance ahead of resolution. Historical mid-May highs average 22°C, but recent cool spells temper upside.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2027 French presidential election's first round set for mid-April 2027—requiring candidates to secure 500 parrainages from elected officials across at least 30 departments—trader sentiment centers on a fragmented field amid post-2024 hung parliament instability. Jean-Luc Mélenchon officially declared his fourth bid last week, joining declared contenders like Édouard Philippe (Horizons) and smaller figures, while Les Républicains nominated Bruno Retailleau three weeks ago. Polls show Jordan Bardella (National Rally) leading first-round voting at 34-38%, boosting expectations for his ballot placement, though Marine Le Pen's ineligibility appeal looms July 7. Upcoming left-wing primary on October 11 could consolidate challengers like Raphaël Glucksmann, with early crowding favoring prominent names but risking signature hurdles for fringes.

markets.question: Will Delcy Rodríguez be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's 19% odds reflect fresh April scandals over his Peter Mandelson appointment and misleading Parliament, spurring resignation calls despite his denials. Israel PM Benjamin Netanyahu trails at 14.8% following late April opposition unity between ex-PMs Bennett and Lapid ahead of October Knesset elections that could oust him. Cuba President Díaz-Canel's position stems from March U.S. demands for his removal in negotiations, which he rejected in April interviews, while Palestinian Authority President Abbas's lower odds tie to his 2025 succession decree without recent shifts. No departures have occurred, keeping the race open to scandals, no-confidence votes, or early elections.

Novak Djokovic sits at 8% amid an early Rome upset loss to Dino Prizmic and ongoing recovery from prior shoulder issues at age 39. Alexander Zverev's 3.9% nods to his No. 3 ranking and Rome progress, while British hopeful Jack Draper's 2.8% gains from home grass advantage and recent upsets like Indian Wells over Djokovic; the field remains wide open on the fast grass surface favoring big servers ahead of Halle and Queen's tune-ups.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has converged on 30.0-34.9% annual inflation for 2026 at a 36.4% implied probability, aligning with the central bank's May 7 survey where analysts lifted end-year forecasts to 30.5%—up 1.4 percentage points—following INDEC's March CPI print of 3.4% monthly, the hottest reading of 2026 and above 3.0% consensus, lifting year-to-date accumulation to 9.4%. Persistent pressures from education (12.1% monthly surge) and transport costs have slowed disinflation under Milei's fiscal austerity, trimming growth outlooks to 2.8% while keeping 25-29.9% (26.0%) and 40-44.9% (24.3%) bins competitive. April CPI, due May 14, represents the pivotal near-term catalyst.

Recent 1-1 draws for Santa Fe versus Corinthians and América de Cali underscore defensive resilience despite key absences like Emmanuel Olivera (quadriceps) and Helibelton Palacios (hamstring) from May 9 injury reports. Platense, second in the group post-1-1 draw at Peñarol, faces its own injury woes including Gonzalo Goñi (Achilles) and Héctor Bobadilla (hamstring), tempering away upset potential at 25.5% while draw trades at 28% reflect the tight head-to-head dynamic.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no in-person Trump-Putin meeting by June 30 (93%), reflecting the absence of any announced bilateral summit or diplomatic venue in the coming seven weeks. A 90-minute phone call on April 29 discussed Ukraine cease-fire prospects and Iran tensions, with Putin signaling openness to post-peace-deal talks with Zelenskyy outside Russia, but Kremlin spokesmen confirmed no immediate plans, including for China. Trump's April invitation for Putin to the December G20 in Miami underscores longer-term engagement without near-term commitments. Logistical hurdles from the Ukraine conflict, sanctions, and alliance pressures persist; a sudden breakthrough like trilateral negotiations or escalation de-escalation could shift odds, though historical patterns show such summits require months of preparation.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: TheMongolz vs G2 - Map 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between TheMongolz and G2 in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 11 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "TheMongolz" if TheMongolz win Map 1 against G2.

This market will resolve to "G2" if G2 win Map 1 against TheMongolz.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: TheMongolz vs G2 (BO3) - PGL Astana Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between TheMongolz and G2 in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 11 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "TheMongolz" if TheMongolz win the match against G2.

This market will resolve to "G2" if G2 win the match against TheMongolz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In PGL Astana 2026 Swiss group stage round 3, both TheMongolz (#8 HLTV) and G2 (#12) hold 1-1 records, positioning this BO3 as pivotal for advancing to 2-1 and bolstering playoff chances. TheMongolz dominated magic 2-0 on day 1 but faltered 0-2 to Spirit yesterday, exposing pistol round vulnerabilities, while G2 cruised past Fisher College 2-0 before dropping 1-2 to MOUZ in a gritty three-mapper. Head-to-head tilts toward TheMongolz at 4-3 overall, bolstered by superior recent form (3rd at PGL Bucharest) and map edges on Ancient (80% win rate) and Dust2. G2's additions like NertZ and MATYS show promise but inconsistency persists; trader consensus sees a razor-thin edge amid fatigue from back-to-back days.
